exampleSentences: Anh muốn ăn thịt bò hay thịt gà cho bữa tối nay?
Mẹ em thường mua thịt tươi ở chợ vào mỗi sáng sớm.
Chị không ăn thịt nên chị chỉ gọi rau và đậu phụ thôi.
notes:
thịt = meat (animal meat you eat).  

It’s a noun. When speaking generally, you just say thịt; for pieces, use words like miếng thịt (a piece), lát thịt (a slice), or measure by weight like một ký thịt.  

Very common pairings: thịt bò (beef), thịt gà (chicken), thịt heo / thịt lợn (pork), thịt vịt (duck).  

It can also mean “flesh” of the body in some contexts (like da thịt = skin and flesh).  

North vs South: pork is thịt lợn in the North, thịt heo in the South — same thing, just different word choice.

content.sentence: Chúng ta cần mua ___ cho bữa tiệc.

translation.en: We need to buy meat for the party.

definitions.en: Meat; the flesh of animals used as food.
definitions.es: Carne; la carne de los animales utilizada como alimento.
definitions.vi: Thịt; phần thịt của động vật được sử dụng làm thực phẩm.

example.en: I like to eat chicken meat.
example.es: Me gusta comer carne de pollo.

translation.en: I like to eat grilled meat.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Do you want to eat beef meat or chicken meat for dinner tonight?
My mom often buys fresh meat at the market every morning.
I do not eat meat so I only order vegetables and tofu.
notes_md:
**thịt** = meat (animal meat you eat).  

It’s a noun. When speaking generally, you just say *thịt*; for pieces, use words like **miếng thịt** (a piece), **lát thịt** (a slice), or measure by weight like **một ký thịt**.  

Very common pairings: **thịt bò** (beef), **thịt gà** (chicken), **thịt heo / thịt lợn** (pork), **thịt vịt** (duck).  

It can also mean “flesh” of the body in some contexts (like **da thịt** = skin and flesh).  

North vs South: pork is **thịt lợn** in the North, **thịt heo** in the South — same thing, just different word choice.
